theme: flickering lantern row follows a secret rule
title: The Flickering Lantern Row
strategies: trivial: guess the identity rule (lanterns never change) -> reproduces the checker's own baseline, ~0.1

greedy: pool every consecutive LOGBOOK pair as if it were one tick apart and majority-vote a radius-1 rule table from the pooled (window -> next-cell) counts; ignores the recorded tick gaps entirely, so on gappy pairs the same 3-window maps to inconsistent outcomes and the vote absorbs noise

strong: constraint satisfaction over the finite 256-rule space -- for each candidate rule, roll it forward EXACTLY the recorded gap between each snapshot pair and score by total mismatch; the rule consistent with every gap simultaneously is (almost always) the true one, recovered without ever fitting a single-step transition directly

headroom above strong: resolve residual ambiguity when sparse/noisy snapshots leave >1 rule near-tied (e.g. weight pairs by gap length, or bootstrap over noise-plausible snapshot reconstructions) to push the held-out 500-tick rollout mismatch closer to the fixed 18% observation-noise floor
